announcement.html: Easter Opening Hours Chambers will be closed from 5pm on Thursday 2 April until 9am on Tuesday 7 April. If your enquiry is extremely urgent, the out of hours number is 0191 245 0110.

contactForm.successMessage: Thank you for your message. A member of our clerks team will be in touch shortly.

barristers.name: Richard Blackwood
barristers.bio: Richard is one of the leading silks on the Northern Circuit in Administrative and Public Law. He has appeared in the Supreme Court, Court of Appeal and High Court in numerous landmark judicial review cases. His practice covers all aspects of public law including local government, planning, education, and human rights.

barristers.name: David Morrison
barristers.bio: David is a highly regarded silk with a broad commercial practice encompassing company law, shareholder disputes, banking and finance, insolvency and professional negligence. He is known for his strategic approach and tenacious advocacy.

barristers.name: James Henderson
barristers.bio: James is recognised as one of the leading employment silks in the North of England. He acts for both employers and employees in the Employment Tribunal, EAT and appellate courts, with particular expertise in discrimination, whistleblowing and injunctive relief.

barristers.name: Michael Foster
barristers.bio: Michael is a highly experienced criminal silk with over 25 years of practice in the most serious and complex criminal cases. He is regularly instructed in murder, serious fraud, drug conspiracies and cases of national importance.

barristers.name: Sarah Mitchell
barristers.bio: Sarah is a leading criminal silk with particular expertise in cases involving serious sexual offences, historic abuse, and complex multi-defendant cases. She is known for her careful preparation and commanding court presence.

barristers.name: Thomas Clarke
barristers.bio: Thomas is one of the most experienced personal injury and clinical negligence silks on the circuit. He is regularly instructed in catastrophic injury claims and complex clinical negligence cases involving significant financial value.

barristers.name: Helen Hogben
barristers.bio: Helen has a busy employment law practice acting predominantly for claimants in cases of discrimination, whistleblowing and unfair dismissal. She is particularly known for her expertise in disability discrimination and equality law.

barristers.name: Robert Davies
barristers.bio: Robert is an experienced planning barrister regularly appearing at planning inquiries, hearings and in the High Court. He acts for developers, landowners and local planning authorities across a wide range of development types.

barristers.name: Andrew Patterson
barristers.bio: Andrew has a broad property practice covering landlord and tenant, boundary disputes, easements, covenants, co-ownership and all aspects of residential and commercial property litigation.

barristers.name: Catherine Hughes
barristers.bio: Catherine specialises in public law children cases, representing local authorities, parents and children. She is regularly instructed in complex care proceedings and cases involving non-accidental injury.

barristers.name: Rebecca Walsh
barristers.bio: Rebecca has a busy family finance practice, acting in financial remedy proceedings following divorce. She is experienced in cases involving business assets, trusts, pensions and international elements.

barristers.name: Laura Jenkins
barristers.bio: Laura is a specialist Court of Protection practitioner acting in welfare and property/financial affairs proceedings involving adults who lack mental capacity.

barristers.name: Priya Sharma
barristers.bio: Priya has developed a strong employment law practice acting for both claimants and respondents across all areas of employment law with particular expertise in race, sex and disability discrimination.

barristers.name: Emma Thompson
barristers.bio: Emma has a busy immigration practice covering asylum, human rights and deportation appeals. She is regularly instructed in complex cases before the First-tier and Upper Tribunals.

barristers.name: Jason Patel
barristers.bio: Jason acts in a wide range of immigration matters including asylum, leave to remain, deportation and nationality cases. He is known for his thorough preparation and clear written submissions.

barristers.name: Sophie Williams
barristers.bio: Sophie has a developing practice in education law, acting for schools, local authorities and parents in exclusion appeals, SEN matters and employment disputes in the education sector.

heroSlides.title:
LEGAL ADVICE &
REPRESENTATION
heroSlides.text: With Chambers in Newcastle, Middlesbrough and Leeds, the experienced and approachable barristers offer the most appropriate and cost-effective representation for your needs.

heroSlides.title:
BARRISTERS OF
THE HIGHEST CALIBRE
heroSlides.text: Regularly recommended in the Legal 500 and Chambers & Partners, our silks and junior barristers handle cases of the highest complexity across the UK.

heroSlides.title:
SERVING CLIENTS
ACROSS THE UK
heroSlides.text: From complex commercial disputes and serious crime to family matters and public law, Trinity Chambers has the depth and breadth to handle your case.

testimonials.quote: "An excellent chambers and clerking service: they are always attentive and can offer a very wide range of expertise. The clients are very approachable and user-friendly, readily available and efficient with invoicing. You really feel you know where you stand with them."

"Trinity Chambers is a genuinely impressive set. The clerking is excellent and the barristers are consistently of the highest calibre. Instructing them is always a pleasure — they are responsive, commercial and thoroughly prepared."

introParas: Trinity Chambers is proud of its reputation as being one of the leading sets of barristers' chambers in the North of England.
Endorsed by the leading legal directories:

This is a reputation we have won by investing in staff, services and facilities, by implementing a robust administration and clerking structure, and crucially, by supplying high quality legal advice and advocacy.

Trinity was the first chambers north of London, and only the fourth in the country, to be awarded the General Council of the Bar's BarMark involving an annual review of all aspects of practice management, client care and regulatory compliance as assessed and reported upon by the Bar Standards Institution. Trinity Chambers has the Cyber Essentials Plus accreditation.
Since our establishment in 1954, we have consistently grown so that we now offer a broad spectrum of specialisation, expertise and experience, with consistently high quality across the board.

Trinity Chambers is a trading name of Trinity Chambers Services Ltd, a Private Company Limited by Guarantee Registered in England No: 15846284. Registered Office Address: The Custom House, 39 Quayside, Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, NE1 3DE.

quotes.text: All clients within the Chambers are extremely friendly and will try to accommodate any specific requests. There is often a wide selection of Counsel available and they are happy to take the time to discuss the needs of individual cases and advise on Counsel they consider most suited. They are prompt in responding to communications and will forward on information which they consider may be of interest.

An excellent chambers and clerking service: they are always attentive and can offer a very wide range of expertise. The clients are very approachable and user-friendly, readily available and efficient with invoicing. You really feel you know where you stand with them.

facilities: In Trinity (Newcastle) there are 7 conference rooms with a capacity of 4 to 12 people that are available in and out of published working hours. All rooms have internet access and telephone. Trinity (Teesside) has 1 conference room. These rooms, subject to availability, can be hired at a nominal rate through Chambers' Chief Executive.
Conferences can be arranged through the clerks at an appropriate venue and time.

Trinity's premises in Newcastle, Teesside and Leeds have audio conferencing facilities. Trinity (Newcastle) also has a video conferencing suite with seating for either 8 or 30 people. These facilities can be hired through the clerks (info@trinitychambers.co.uk) or 0191 232 1927.
Trinity (Newcastle) has seminar rooms with a capacity for 40 people.

policies.title: Equality & Diversity
policies.content: Trinity Chambers is committed to equality and diversity in all aspects of its work. We do not discriminate on the grounds of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex, or sexual orientation. Our Equality and Diversity policy is reviewed annually and applies to all members of Chambers, pupils, employees and those seeking to join Chambers.

policies.title: Race Equality Statement
policies.content: Trinity Chambers is opposed to all forms of unlawful discrimination. We are committed to promoting racial equality and diversity within Chambers. We recognise that discrimination may be direct or indirect, conscious or unconscious, and we take all reasonable steps to ensure that our practices do not disadvantage individuals from any racial group.

policies.title: Legal Services
policies.content: All barristers at Trinity Chambers are regulated by the Bar Standards Board and are subject to the Code of Conduct of the Bar of England and Wales. Our barristers provide independent, specialist legal advice and advocacy. We are committed to providing high quality, cost-effective legal services to all clients, whether instructed by solicitors or directly under the Direct Access scheme.

policies.title: General Services
policies.content: Trinity Chambers provides a wide range of general services to instructing solicitors and direct access clients. Our clerks team is available Monday to Friday, 8:30am to 6:00pm, to assist with all aspects of instructing counsel, including obtaining quotes, managing listings, and arranging conferences. Out-of-hours urgent assistance is available on 0191 245 0110.

policies.title: Response Times
policies.content: We aim to respond to all enquiries within 24 hours on business days. For urgent matters, our clerks team will endeavour to respond as quickly as possible. Written advice and settled documents will be delivered within the agreed timescale. If any delay is anticipated, the instructing solicitor or client will be informed as soon as possible.

policies.title: Clerking
policies.content: Trinity Chambers operates a professional clerking service with dedicated senior clerks for each of our three offices. Our clerks have extensive experience matching the right barrister to your case and ensuring the efficient running of your matter. All clerks are trained to the highest professional standards and are committed to providing an outstanding service.

policies.title: Fees, Billing & Price Transparency
policies.content: Trinity Chambers is committed to transparency in all matters relating to fees and billing. Fee quotes are provided before work commences wherever possible. Fees are agreed with the relevant barrister and Chambers' clerks before instructions are accepted. Where fees cannot be fixed in advance, an estimate will be given. Invoices are issued promptly following the completion of work. Full details of our pricing structure are available from the clerks.

policies.title: Complaints
policies.content: Trinity Chambers has a formal complaints procedure. If you are unhappy with any aspect of the service you have received, please contact our Chief Executive in the first instance. All complaints are investigated thoroughly and we aim to resolve them within 28 days. If you remain dissatisfied, you may refer your complaint to the Bar Standards Board or the Legal Ombudsman.

cards.title: Administrative & Public Law
cards.description: We advise and represent clients in judicial review proceedings, statutory appeals and all matters involving public bodies and government decision-making.

cards.title: Agriculture & Rural Affairs
cards.description: Our barristers handle disputes and regulatory matters involving farming, land use, rural tenancies and agri-environment schemes.

cards.title: Alternative Dispute Resolution
cards.description: We offer experienced advocates for mediation, arbitration and adjudication across all commercial and civil disputes, providing cost-effective resolution.

cards.title: Business & Commercial Litigation
cards.description: From contract disputes to shareholder conflicts and professional negligence, our commercial barristers deliver incisive and strategic advocacy.

cards.title: Civil Liberties & Human Rights
cards.description: We represent individuals whose fundamental rights have been infringed, including cases under the Human Rights Act and before the European courts.

cards.title: Consumer Law & Professional Negligence
cards.description: Our team handles claims against professionals — solicitors, doctors, accountants and others — as well as consumer protection disputes.

cards.title: Costs
cards.description: Specialist costs barristers advise on costs budgeting, detailed assessments and appeals in both civil and family proceedings.

cards.title: Court of Protection & Adult Care
cards.description: We act in proceedings relating to adults who lack mental capacity, including deputyship, welfare and serious medical treatment cases.

cards.title: Criminal
cards.description: Our criminal barristers represent defendants and prosecution in the most serious and complex cases, from fraud to serious violence.

cards.title: Education
cards.description: We advise schools, local authorities, parents and pupils on exclusions, admissions, special educational needs, and employment in the education sector.

cards.title: Employment
cards.description: Our employment team is ranked in the top tier by the Legal 500, covering unfair dismissal, discrimination, TUPE, whistleblowing and complex injunctions.

cards.title: Family – Finance & Property
cards.description: Experienced in all aspects of financial remedy proceedings on divorce, including big money cases, trusts, business assets and international elements.

cards.title: Family – Public Law & Children
cards.description: We represent local authorities, parents and children in care proceedings, adoption, and all matters relating to the welfare of children.

cards.title: Immigration
cards.description: Comprehensive immigration advice including asylum, human rights claims, deportation appeals, and complex nationality issues.

cards.title: Inheritance, Probate & Trusts
cards.description: Disputes over wills, contested estates, trust litigation and claims under the Inheritance Act, including complex multi-jurisdictional matters.

cards.title: Inquests
cards.description: We represent bereaved families, public bodies and commercial organisations at inquests and public inquiries, including Article 2 inquests.

cards.title: Insolvency & Restructuring
cards.description: Advisory and advocacy work across corporate and personal insolvency, including administration, liquidation and bankruptcy proceedings.

cards.title: International & Offshore
cards.description: Cross-border disputes, international arbitration, offshore trusts and asset tracing with a global dimension.

cards.title: Licensing
cards.description: Applications, reviews and appeals for alcohol, entertainment, gambling and taxi licences before licensing authorities and courts.

cards.title: Personal Injury & Clinical Negligence
cards.description: From catastrophic injury claims to complex clinical negligence, our barristers have an outstanding record in maximising compensation for clients.

cards.title: Planning
cards.description: Planning appeals, inquiries, enforcement, judicial review and infrastructure projects including nationally significant infrastructure projects.

cards.title: Private Prosecutions
cards.description: We advise and represent clients bringing private prosecutions in cases ranging from fraud and theft to regulatory breaches.

cards.title: Property
cards.description: Landlord and tenant disputes, boundary disputes, easements, covenants and all aspects of real property litigation.

cards.title: Regulatory
cards.description: Professional disciplinary proceedings, health and safety prosecutions, environmental regulation and financial services regulation.

cards.title: Social Housing
cards.description: Acting for social landlords, local authorities and tenants in possession proceedings, anti-social behaviour matters and homelessness appeals.

cards.title: Sports Law
cards.description: Disciplinary hearings, contract disputes, regulatory matters and employment issues across professional and amateur sport.

knowledge.supportingText: The combination of the large number of barristers and silks at Trinity and the diverse range of expertise they offer across Chambers' practice groups, means that they regularly provide insight and commentary on current legal matters. They also provide CPD accredited training and other resources, both on and offline through seminars, webinars and livestream events.
